neighborhoods.name: Old Venetian Harbor (Chania)
neighborhoods.description: Stunning Venetian harbor with lighthouse, converted arsenali, and one of Greece's most atmospheric old towns
neighborhoods.character: Venetian harbor, atmospheric, walkable old town

housing.predominantType: Apartments in Venetian and Ottoman-era buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Chania. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Akrotiri
neighborhoods.description: Peninsula east of Chania with beaches, monasteries, and a mix of residential and tourist development
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Chania's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Souda
neighborhoods.description: Port town east of Chania with naval base, lower costs, and ferry connections to Piraeus
neighborhoods.character: Port town, affordable, practical transit hub
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Apokoronas (Almyrida/Vamos)
neighborhoods.description: Coastal and hillside villages east of Chania popular with British and Northern European expats
neighborhoods.character: Village coast, expat community, traditional Cretan

housing.predominantType: Restored stone houses, villas, and new-build apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
